one_line: Pure black/white, Geist Sans + Geist Mono. Developer-native by default. Feels like Vercel, Cursor, Resend, the Geist design system.

background.role: Pure black. Yes, pure — this profile is the exception that allows it.

surface.role: Elevated surface, one notch from pure black.

text_primary.role: Near-pure white for text. Slightly off to reduce eye strain.

border.role: Default borders. Subtle but visible against pure black.

accent.role: Primary action is WHITE on black. Inversion is the brand signature.

accent_contrast.role: Text color on the accent button — pure black.

accent.role: Inverted — black on white in light mode.

rules: This is the ONE profile where pure #000 and #FFF are correct — they're the brand signature
Primary button INVERTS: white-on-black in dark mode, black-on-white in light mode
Use mono for tabular numbers, code snippets, hashes, IDs, deploy URLs, anything technical-feeling
Single accent across modes. Semantic colors (green/amber/red) used as 1-2px indicators, never as backgrounds

display.rule: Geist headlines use TIGHT tracking — much tighter than Inter would. -0.04em or even -0.05em on large sizes.

mono.family: Geist Mono, Berkeley Mono, JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace
mono.rule: Use mono AGGRESSIVELY. This profile is recognizable by how much monospace appears in unexpected places (button hints, footer detail, navigation breadcrumbs with arrows).

rules: Geist over Inter when available — the proportions are slightly different and feel more 'Vercel'
Headlines: weight 600-700, tight tracking, often ALL CAPS variants for tiny labels
Dense by default, but marketing pages allow 64-96px section gutters
Default radius: md (6px). Vercel uses small radii — never pills
Code blocks: sm (4px). Inline code chips: sm (4px)
Almost no shadows. Borders carry the weight.
Modals/popovers only: 0 0 0 1px border, 0 12px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.5) in dark, rgba(0,0,0,0.08) in light
Never glow effects except on focus rings

button_primary.rule: The inversion IS the design. Don't add gradients, don't add shadows.

input_text.border_focus_glow: none — Vercel doesn't glow on focus
input_text.rule: Focus is border becoming pure white/black. No accent glow.

code_chip.rule: Use these EVERYWHERE for technical refs. Vercel's marketing has like 50 of these per page.

marketing_hero.structure: Centered hero, max-width 800px. Headline in Geist 600-weight, 4xl-5xl, tight tracking. One-line subhead in body. Two CTAs (primary inverted + ghost). Below: a real product shot (screenshot or animated UI), full-width but contained.
do: Mono accents in copy: 'Deploy in 30 seconds — npm install vercel'
Code snippets as the visual proof, not illustrations
dont: Loose tracking on large type — looks unfinished

product_dashboard.structure: Left sidebar (240px) with project list. Top breadcrumb in mono. Content area is cards with 1px borders. Tables use tabular-nums; every numeric column right-aligned.
do: Deploy IDs and commits in mono (text-mono text-xs)
Status badges as tiny pills with 1px border, not solid fills
dont: Stock 'illustration of person at laptop' images

docs.structure: Three-column: left nav (240px), content (max 720px), right TOC (200px). Code blocks dominate the content area. Headlines in Geist, tight tracking.
do: Code blocks with the surface_subtle bg and 1px border

animations.philosophy: Even more restrained than linear-product. Dev tools shouldn't pretend to be marketing pages.

patterns.hover: 150ms color tween. Borders shift to text_primary on focused inputs — no glow.
patterns.deploy_status: Subtle pulse animation on 'deploying' status (2s loop, opacity 0.6→1.0).
patterns.code_copy: Brief flash of accent color on the 'copied' confirmation, 800ms total fade-out.

anti_patterns: any 'wow' moment in the product UI
